crime.value: Murder of Aloysius Garcia at Wisteria Lodge, with mysterious disappearance of host and household
crime.quote: One of them is dead. Did his companions follow him and kill him?

victim.value: Aloysius Garcia, likely targeted due to his connection to San Pedro political conspiracy
victim.quote: The man was a primitive savage from the backwoods of San Pedro

closed_world.value: Wisteria Lodge house and its immediate grounds where the crime occurred
closed_world.quote: some very strange people with some very strange ways in this house

culprit_and_motive.value: Garcia's companions from San Pedro, motivated by political conspiracy or betrayal
culprit_and_motive.quote: Did his companions follow him and kill him? If they did we should have them, for every port is watched

resolution.value: Holmes solves the case through deduction and research into voodoo practices, explaining the ritual elements
resolution.quote: I spent a morning in the British Museum reading up on that and other points

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes as the primary detective, with Watson as narrator and Inspector Baynes as official police
investigator.quote: Holmes smiled and rubbed his hands. 'I must congratulate you, Inspector, on handling so distinctive'

suspect_architecture.value: Mulatto cook (primary suspect with means/motive/opportunity), mysterious companions who fled, Scott Eccles (false suspect with alibi), Inspector Baynes working competing theory
suspect_architecture.quote: One of them is dead. Did his companions follow him and kill him? If they did we should have them

red_herring.value: Scott Eccles positioned as initial suspect with his 'grotesque experience' and connection to the house, deflecting from the real culprits
red_herring.quote: Have just had most incredible and grotesque experience. May I consult you?

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence presented equally to reader and Holmes - charred bones, blood, torn bird, voodoo ritual elements described in detail
clue_fairness.quote: Then from the table he took a platter heaped with small pieces of charred bone

social_world.value: Colonial power dynamics - primitive savage from San Pedro backwoods vs British institutional authority, exotic otherness as threat
social_world.quote: The man was a primitive savage from the backwoods of San Pedro, and this was his fetish

reveal_mechanic.value: Academic research validation - Holmes consults British Museum and quotes Eckermann's text on voodoo practices to decode ritual evidence
reveal_mechanic.quote: I spent a morning in the British Museum reading up on that and other points

media_and_audience.value: Grotesque framed as stepping stone to horrible - public fascination with exotic supernatural practices domesticated through scholarly explanation
media_and_audience.quote: It is grotesque, Watson, but there is but one step from the grotesque to the horrible

investigator_wound.value: Holmes's intellectual boredom and racing mind seeking stimulation - vulnerability to cases that promise mental engagement over routine
investigator_wound.quote: My mind is like a racing engine, tearing itself to pieces because it is not connected up with the work

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle the adventure of wisteria lodge
